people.text: Isabel wants a flat outdoor walk for her grandfather. He cannot walk far and needs a café nearby.
Marco is taking two teenagers on a rainy day. They want indoor physical challenges, not a museum.
Lena loves old buildings and wants a guided afternoon visit where she can hear stories about the past.
Sam wants a cheap place for a picnic with friends. They need space for ball games and bus access.
Aisha wants to photograph city views at sunset. She does not mind walking uphill.

texts.text: River Garden has [6]flat paths[/6], benches every few minutes and a small [6]café beside the entrance[/6]. The shortest route takes about twenty minutes and is suitable for visitors who tire easily.
Climb Hall is an [7]indoor centre[/7] with rope bridges, low climbing walls and team challenges. It is popular with [7]teenagers[/7], especially when the weather is wet.
Castle Stories offers [8]guided afternoon tours[/8] of the old castle. Guides explain how people lived there and share local legends connected with each room.
Green Park is [9]free to enter[/9] and has open grass for [9]football or volleyball[/9]. Several buses stop nearby, and picnic tables are available near the lake.
Hilltop Viewpoint is reached by a steep path, but photographers often go there for [10]wide city views[/10] and warm [10]sunset light[/10].
The Aquarium is indoors and has displays of tropical fish, but visitors must book expensive tickets online.
Lakeside Trail is beautiful, but parts are muddy and too steep for visitors with limited mobility.
Skyline Café has excellent views through its windows, but photography is not allowed inside.

answers.explanation: Isabel needs flat paths and a nearby café.
Marco needs indoor physical challenges for teenagers.
Lena wants an afternoon guided visit to an old building.
Sam needs a cheap picnic place with games and buses.
Aisha wants sunset city views and can walk uphill.
